prompt_md:
Your team is adding a feature that **converts a free-text customer request into a polished email reply** that a human agent edits before sending. A separate part of the screen must also show the customer's **exact account balance**, pulled from your database.

You are deciding how to source the **reply-drafting** part. AI reply-drafting is **not** your product's differentiator, you have **no labeled dataset** yet, and you want to ship quickly while keeping the freedom to change providers later.

Which sourcing decision best fits this situation — and why?

decision_choices: Use AI to compute the exact account balance as well, to keep one consistent technology
Build a custom model in-house now, since owning the model is always cheapest long-term
Call a hosted LLM API behind a thin internal abstraction layer for the reply drafting, keeping the balance as a deterministic database query
Buy the most proprietary end-to-end SaaS suite available so the vendor handles everything
reason_choices: Because the reply drafting is fuzzy language work that fits AI and a human edits it, while the exact balance is a known lookup best left to deterministic code — and an abstraction layer keeps vendor lock-in and switching costs low
Because AI gives the same answer every time, so it is ideal for computing an exact balance
Because building in-house has the lowest total cost of ownership in every case
Because higher vendor lock-in reduces switching costs and is therefore desirable
